assertions.description: The CSSNestedDeclarations interface is exposed on the global object.
CSSNestedDeclarations.prototype is an instance of CSSRule (it's a nested-declarations rule).
Bare declarations following a nested rule are preserved as a CSSNestedDeclarations rule rather than dropped.
A CSSNestedDeclarations rule exposes a `style` CSSStyleDeclaration like other declaration carriers.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const s=document.createElement('style');s.textContent='.a{ color:red; &.b{color:blue;} background:white; }';document.head.appendChild(s);const top=s.sheet.cssRules[0];const nd=Array.from(top.cssRules||[]).find(r=>r.constructor.name==='CSSNestedDeclarations');const ok=!!(nd && nd.style && nd.style.background);s.remove();return ok;}catch(err){return false;}})()

assertions.description: CSS nesting itself (the & selector) parses, the precondition for this feature.
